description:
<title>Retrieve local backup status via curl and archive it</title>
    <role>You are a database reliability engineer verifying that last-night’s back-up finished correctly.</role>

    <description>
        A mock “backup status” API response is already staged on the system as a local
        file-URL endpoint:

            file:///home/user/mock_backup_api.json

        Your job is to archive an exact byte-for-byte copy of that JSON payload.

        Requirements the automated checker will enforce:
          1. You must use curl (any valid flag combination is accepted) to retrieve that
             file-URL.
          2. The payload must be written to the *exact* path

                 /home/user/backup_archive/last_backup.json

             • If intermediate directories do not yet exist you must create them.
             • The resulting file must be an exact checksum match to the source data; no
               extra characters, newlines, or transformations are allowed.
          3. The curl invocation (including any redirection pipes) must be accomplished in
             *one* shell command line.  
             • “One” means everything appears on a single prompt line; logical
               operators ( && | ; ) are permitted if you need them.
          4. Your command should run quietly: pass curl’s “silent” option so no
             progress meter or headers show up.
          5. You do not need to display the file afterwards; the automated test will read
             it directly.

        Output expectations:
            • The terminal must stay silent except for any text you yourself
              intentionally print (optional).  The JSON itself must not be echoed to
              stdout.
            • The file /home/user/backup_archive/last_backup.json must exist and be
              byte-identical to /home/user/mock_backup_api.json when the command
              finishes.

        Once the single command has executed successfully, you’re done.
    </description>
truth:
<!-- Pre-existing asset that the agent has to fetch -->
    <pre_existing_file path="/home/user/mock_backup_api.json"><![CDATA[
{
  "backup_id": "2024-06-15T08:30:00Z",
  "status": "completed",
  "duration_seconds": 125,
  "size_bytes": 10485760
}
]]></pre_existing_file>

    <!-- Item the checker will verify after the agent runs -->
    <expected_output_file path="/home/user/backup_archive/last_backup.json" must_equal_source="/home/user/mock_backup_api.json"/>
name: task_000000_3e3a00ed
